scenario.context: Station 3 of the day: build and connect the workstation.
scenario.mission: You build a standard station ready for operation and verify basic functionality.

station.profile: Desktop, 2 monitors, keyboard, mouse, LAN, headset

customerRequest.subject: Workstation setup for station NS-ST-03
customerRequest.body:
Please build station NS-ST-03 completely and check basic functionality.

Important: correct cabling, working peripherals, and a short setup log.

questions.title: Mandatory points during setup
questions.prompt: Which points are part of the basic equipment for an office workstation? (Multiple selection)

options.text: Connect and test keyboard and mouse

options.rationale: Not part of the technical baseline.

options.text: Leave cables loosely behind the desk

feedback.correct: Correct: you focus on the technically required setup.
feedback.wrong: Partially incorrect: separate the mandatory scope more clearly.

questions.title: Sequence for workstation setup
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps in a sensible order.

feedback.correct: Correct: the setup is structured and traceable.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: plan/connect first, then test and documentation.

questions.title: First measure for cable chaos
questions.prompt: During testing you notice that cables cannot be assigned. What is the best first step?

options.text: Ignore everything as long as the PC runs
Route cables cleanly, assign them and label briefly

options.rationale: Does not solve the actual problem.

feedback.correct: Correct: structured cabling is part of quality.
feedback.wrong: Not fitting: the issue is missing assignment.

questions.title: Setup time planning
questions.prompt: A setup takes 35 minutes per workstation. For 4 workstations add 20 minutes for the final check. How many minutes should be planned in total?

feedback.wrong: Please add setup time per station and final check.

questions.title: Setup log
questions.prompt: Write a short log (4-5 sentences) with setup status, test and an open item.
questions.expectedOutline: Short report with status, test result and, if applicable, a remaining item for follow-up.

feedback.correct: Correct: the log is practically usable.
feedback.wrong: Please state status, test reference and open item more clearly.
